codeReview.summary: Prerequisite fix for M5-pull-push: adds GitError.remoteRejected, .hookRejected, .protectedBranch with detail extractors (hookDeclinedReason, protectedBranchDetail, remoteRejectedReason) and places the specific push-rejection patterns BEFORE the generic nonFastForward check so a server-side 'denying non-fast-forward' classifies as .remoteRejected rather than an incorrect local-side hint. ErrorClassifier now has three dedicated extractors that pull an actionable detail line from multi-line stderr (the 'Changes must be made through a pull request.' GH006 follow-up, or the tail of 'hook declined: <reason>'). Parameterised fixture suite + detail-extraction tests + 2 explicit precedence assertions. Clean, well-justified ordering.

issues.description: Auth-pattern ordering still mis-classifies a MISSING-REMOTE-DIR fetch as .auth because 'could not read from remote repository' fires before the .notAGitRepository check at line 91. M5-fetch's discoveredIssue flags this: NetworkOpsFetchTests.fetchReportsErrorOnMissingRemote produces an 'Authentication failed' toast for a deleted bare. The fix is a two-line re-order — move the 'does not appear to be a git repository' check ABOVE the auth block. Deferred; not introduced by this feature (pattern predates it) and user-facing impact is mild (toast still shows full stderr via click-to-expand). Queue as M8 polish.

'not a git repository' case-insensitive match catches both the remote and local variants (e.g. a 'fatal: not a git repository' thrown by a local git invocation with a wrong cwd). Harmless today but worth a comment noting this is intentional; future readers may try to narrow it to the remote-side variant. One-line doc comment suffices.

sharedStateObservations.observation: GitError now carries 3 push-rejection cases (remoteRejected / hookRejected / protectedBranch) with per-case LocalizedError strings tuned for toast rendering. Downstream NetworkOps.pushErrorMessage consumes them via a switch/default pattern — consistent.
Classifier now has explicit comments documenting the 'specific before generic' rule (ErrorClassifier.swift:38-42, 59-60). Subsequent workers touching ErrorClassifier will see the discipline.
